description.task: Implement an ArrayOperations class with methods for inserting an element at a specific position and deleting an element by value in a logical array (tracked by a 'size' field, backed by a fixed array). Return the new logical size after each operation.
instructions: insert(pos, value): shift elements at indices size-1..pos one position right, set data[pos]=value, increment size, return new size
delete(value): find first index i where data[i]==value; shift elements from i+1..size-1 one position left; decrement size; return new size. Return -1 if not found
Both methods must respect the capacity guard (given in insert) — do not add elements beyond data.length
print() is already implemented and prints only the logical portion (0..size-1)
After fill([1,2,3,4,5]), insert(2,99) makes [1,2,99,3,4,5]; then delete(99) restores [1,2,3,4,5]
description.hint: insert: for(int i=size-1;i>=pos;i--) data[i+1]=data[i]; data[pos]=value; size++; return size. delete: find idx, then for(int i=idx;i<size-1;i++) data[i]=data[i+1]; size--; return size;

description.task: Implement a SearchOperations class with linear search that returns both the index and the number of comparisons made. Also implement findAll() that returns all indices where a value appears, and countOccurrences().
instructions: searchWithCount(arr, target): iterate arr; increment comparisons each step; when arr[i]==target return {i, comparisons}; after loop return {-1, comparisons}
findAll(arr, target): iterate all elements, add index to list when arr[i]==target
countOccurrences uses findAll — do not modify it
Array {3,1,4,1,5,9,2,6,1}: searching for 4 finds it at index 2 with 3 comparisons
Searching for 7 (not in array) requires 9 comparisons
description.hint: searchWithCount: int comparisons=0; for(int i=0;i<arr.length;i++){ comparisons++; if(arr[i]==target) return new int[]{i,comparisons}; } return new int[]{-1,comparisons};
description.expectedOutput:
Index: 2, Comparisons: 3
Index: -1, Comparisons: 9
All indices of 1: [1, 3, 8]
Count of 1: 3

description.task: Implement a TraversalPatterns class with three traversal methods: forward (left-to-right), backward (right-to-left), and alternating (first from ends moving inward). Each method returns the elements in the visiting order as an int array.
instructions: backward(arr): create result[n], fill from index n-1 down to 0 reading from arr left-to-right (or equivalently, read arr right-to-left into result left-to-right)
alternating(arr): use left=0, right=arr.length-1, and a result index k; while left<=right, add arr[left++] then (if left<=right) add arr[right--] to result
All return arrays must have length == arr.length
forward() is already implemented as a reference for backward()
description.hint: backward: for(int i=arr.length-1;i>=0;i--) result[arr.length-1-i]=arr[i]; alternating: int k=0,l=0,r=arr.length-1; while(l<=r){result[k++]=arr[l++]; if(l<=r)result[k++]=arr[r--];}
